Still think it's such a shame that these were made unlisted, and even more so that one was made private and is so unviewable. Tnk used to be private, but he changed it to unlisted at my request, which I do very much appreciate. text: This one seems to be about a Red heavy encountering a medic who was once a friend, but who has seemingly turned to the Blue team. The medic taunts and the heavy realizes he must destroy his former friend. Some super-saiyan maneuver is performed which is intended to destroy the medic at the cost of the heavy's own life. During this, the medic remembers his life with Red team and - realizing their imminent mortality - extends his hand in friendship so that they may die together as friends reunited

text:
"Nostalgia" was considered a medical condition in the 18th century, and was much more akin to homesickness than how we use the word today. The word can be literally translated to "the pain of homecoming." This video captures that nostalgia at its most painful, and that's why it's still one of my favorites.
Lots of good TF2 video makers out there, but nobody was ever as good as Gzero.
(pretentious comment over, go about your business)

text: The medic is a demigod, with the ability to grant lost power to other people. Notice the soldier in the last photo, he has one arm. He is the armless soldier from previous videos. Medic restored his arm to him, in the same way he restored the arm of the stone sniper. Arms represent power, which is why Schniper has 3 arms in his confrontation with Spiritual Demoman, to show his increased power. Spiritual Demoman's twisted fingers show he has mastered control over his power.

text:
I've rewatched this probably a thousand times already. Still doesn't get any less epic.

Gzero91, it's a shame that you've stopped and that no new epic fragments of dreams will be captured in a TF2 gmod video anymore. I'll genuinely miss those crazy characters, that great camerawork and the brilliant music that always fitted the theme and action exactly.

I'm pretty sure though that some day I will watch some new work of you, only then probably not made on gmod, and shown on a far bigger screen.

text:
"As the song builds and the chorus refrains, before you know it, before you can stop it, it all comes to a crescendo; an incredible realization of everything that came before, a moment of clarity. And it rings, and it roars, and it's the loudest sound you've ever heard, and then...

...It's... just... gone.

...The song is over, and all you're left with is... silence."


We'll miss your TF2 videos, Gzero. Good luck with whatever it is you choose to do.

author: @Blashbuck

text: Medic and heavy shared a close friendship, until the medic decided to betray the reds and join the blues. Heavy was heartbroken,  and decided to die, and take medic with him. Medic, realizing the pain he'd brought and his role in breaking the red team apart, asked for forgiveness in the form of a handshake. He then sent heavy back to the surface and faced his fate alone. The spiritual demo man then comes to accompany medic onward, and saves him from the influence of the schniper.
author: @Blashbuck

text:
I've just realized that all of these videos are all one story.

author: @ButtslammerBob

text: I just watched all your gmod videos, with this as the last one. It was like watching an epic movie with an epic and emotional ending... I'm subbed for life!
author: @Konfektionery

text:
I really wish half the sincere, emotional artistic talent you have. I'm currently writing some emotional scenes for a series, and I'm just finding it hard to just write real emotion without it being cheesy, animated, and over-dramatic. But this, combined with the score, is just perfection. the honest stuff film-makers with millions of dollars in their budget strive for, but rarely attain.

Honestly one of my favorite videos from you, or TF2. Thank you for your hard work and being an inspiration.

text:
2:48 - 2:55

The demoman comes to finish the Medic. To end the corruption.

3:00 - End
A memory, of what once was.. but is now destroyed.
author: @tagger324

text:
1:28 - 2:09
He reaches towards Heavy, as a sort of apology. Almost as if he is telling him that he knows he has fucked up and that what the Heavy is doing is the only way to fix it. He pushes Heavy away and chooses to face his own fate alone..

2:12 - 2:33 As Medic falls back down to death, he awaits death. He hears the screams of the killed and feels remorse for it..
author: @tagger324

text:
0:00 - 0:40
Heavy tries one last time to convince Medic back to his senses. He misses his friend, he doesn't want to hurt him. Medic chooses to ignore his reasoning and proceeds to taunt him anyway. Heavy charges Medic and lifts him to the sky, there was no other choice..

0:43 - 1:25
As the sparring duo makes their way back to earth, the Medic flashes back. He realizes how much damage he has done to what was once his family. He drove them all insane. He was corrupted. He finally understands.
author: @tagger324

text:
You've got the ability to capture deeply emotional and beautiful sequences, but unless they follow some logical narrative, the audience will have trouble being able understand what's beautiful about it.

text:
Yeah it's hard. Very hard. I've tried it. But it doesn't take 36 hours.
Stop motion animators such as antoine35DeLak deserve their views, no doubt about it. You can tell they put a LOT of work into it. But if I made my characters slide across the floor with extremely crude face posing then it could take me maybe 4 hours maximum to make a "high quality" stop motion (in RubberFruit's standards).

If you think 4 hours of taking pictures is hard work then you must see antoine35DeLak as a God.
author: @RageAndQuit

text:
@Argodaemon I agree with you 100%. Perhaps that's why the view count is so high. Because people forget about it, they go back to watch it.

I'm not like that though. If I don't enjoy something as much as this (for example, I don't watch it again. But if it does, as you say, stick with me, then I return to watch it again. I'm not even kidding. I've watched this another time along with the 3 last night because your comments brought me back. Now I must watch it again.
author: @RageAndQuit

text:
@Robosnop Well then I guess it shows how simplistic the audience of RubberFruit is. You could throw cushions together and add a fire and they would clap about how it's "good entertainment". No thanks. I like a spine chilling storyline with red herrings and turns in the plot. Not characters spontaneously appearing out of nowhere and dropping from ceilings, exploding randomly, and eating each other.

There's being random. And then there's being RubberFruit. They're NOT the same thing.

text: Heavy's long trip has finally led him to his old friend, Medic. A traitor to his team, Medic's frightening past resurfaces as he faces death at his friend's hand- all manipulated by Schniper's dark will. RED is erased. Then, a miracle happens. Medic's heart is changed at the last moment, touched by Heavy's unshakable friendship. Medic's body hurtles through the clouds, directly into the path of Schniper and Spiritual Demo's final battleground. Hope returns to RED's past, present and future.

text:
So after losing all his partners, he was being haunted by the memory of his former friend, the Medic. Not wanting to accept the demise of the Medic, the Heavy wanted to rid his memories of the loss of his partners and Medic.

But as he was slowly strangling away one past moment after the other, the Heavy began remember the moments of gladness and not the sad end.

The cherished moments of their journey, not the unfortunate end.

Realizing this, the Heavy accepted both their, and his own fate.

author: @NigelGriff

text:
Oh my god, It all makes sense....
Every character in these videos were once part of the same RED team. Medic and Heavy  once together before Pyro killed Heavy, and Medic defected to BLU. That's why Red Soldier hates the Blu Demo so much, because he betrayed him. It also explains why Sniper has such a breakdown in Ealiinue. He's a blank slate and doesn't know whether to join the stronger BLU team or his old friendly RED team, and undergoes a huge identity crisis.
